channel.title: 今日のギャグフィード
channel.description: Google Gemini APIによって毎日自動生成される今日のギャグフィードです。

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-13) 829
item.description: ねぇ、猫が車に乗ったら何になると思う？ カーネーション！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-13) 1707
item.description: ダミーのギャグ：アルミ缶の上にあるみかん！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-13) 1580
item.description: この前買ったお煎餅、美味しくて食べ始めたら止まらなくて、結局「全滅」しちゃったよ！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-13) 851
item.description: 「この魚、新鮮だね！どこで獲れたの？」「イカがですか？」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-13) 202
item.description: ダミーのギャグ：アルミ缶の上にあるみかん！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-13) 570
item.description: 今日の天気は晴れと曇り、どっちかハッキリしないなぁ。まるで俺の親父ギャグみたいだろ？「スベる」か「ウケる」か、いつもハッキリしないんだ！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 369
item.description: 「このレモン、酸っぱいだけじゃなくて、疲労回復にもいいんだって？まさに『疲労』に『レモン』だね！」

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 657
item.description: ねぇ、今日のランチ、何食べる？んー、ラーメンにし「ようかん」？

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 323
item.description:
[THINK] The user wants a funny "oyaji gag" in Japanese. I need to create one and output *only* the text of the gag, no extra explanations or greetings.
Let's brainstorm some typical oyaji gag structures: 1. **Pun on similar-sounding words:** This is the most common. 2. **Misinterpretation/play on words with double meanings.** 3. **Relating something to food/drink.** 4. **Slightly absurd, self-deprecating.**
Let's try to make a pun. What are some common topics or words? - 食べ物 (food) - 動物 (animals) - 場所 (places) - 行動 (actions)
Let's try with a common action or situation. Example: 電車 (train) - 「電車が遅れてるね」 - 「うん、ちょっとデンジャー（危険）だね」 - A bit too simple, maybe not funny enough.
How about something related to a common object or tool? ドライバー (screwdriver) - 「このドライバー、ネジを締めるのに便利だね。」 - 「うん、でも使いすぎると、体がスクリュードライバーになるぞ！」 (Doesn't make sense.)
Let's try a classic setup: asking a question and giving a pun answer.
Question: What do you call something...? 何とかと何とかをかけて。
Consider common Japanese sounds/words that can be played with. "ある日" (aru hi - one day) -> "アルヒ" (Al Hí, like "アルフィー" The Alfee, but not quite a gag)
What about "〜がない" (〜 ga nai - doesn't have/is missing)? - 「スマホがない！」 - 「それは、スマホ（スマート）じゃないな。」 (Hmm, a bit weak.)
Let's try focusing on a particular word that has a similar-sounding homophone or a word that can be split to make a pun.
How about something related to "犬" (inu - dog)? - 「この犬、お座りできるんだね。」 - 「ああ、お利口さんだ。でも、エサをあげないとイヌ（要らない）って言われちゃうかもな！」 (A bit forced.)
Let's try a simple setup about a person's name or a common situation. What about "パン" (pan - bread)? - 「このパン、美味しいね！」 - 「うん、でも食べすぎると、お腹がパンパン（いっぱい）になっちゃうぞ！」 (Too obvious, maybe not a "gag" but just a common phrase.)
Let's try a play on words with a common situation and a foreign loanword or a kanji word.
How about something related to being tired or sleep? 「眠いね」 (Nemui ne - I'm sleepy) - 「え？もしかして、枕がメヌイ（フランス語でメニュー）？」 (No, too obscure and doesn't fit the flow.)
Let's go for a classic pun structure: "〇〇って、〇〇なんだよね～"
Consider a situation where someone is struggling or working hard. 「頑張る」 (ganbaru - to do one's best)
How about a pun using "パン" (bread) but in a different context?
「このパン、焼きたてでフワフワだね！」 「うん、まるでパンのふかふかベッドみたいだね。」 「え？パンがベッド？じゃあ、寝坊しちゃったらどうするの？」 「そりゃあ…『寝パン』になっちゃうな！」 (寝パン is 'sleeping bread', but also could sound like 'netapan' - ネタパン -> ネタ (joke) + パン (bread), or just sounds silly. But a bit too convoluted.)
Let's try to make it simpler and more direct.
What about a pun on a common object or food? "梨" (nashi - pear) "無し" (nashi - none/without)
"最近、運動不足で体力が…梨（なし）だね。" "え？梨？" "うん、だから運動して、体力を…あり（有り）にするぞ！" (A bit long-winded)
Let's try a word that can be easily split into two common words. "布団" (futon - Japanese mattress/bedding) "ふとん" -> "ふと" (suddenly) + "ん" (sound) "ふと" (ふと - suddenly) + "ON" (オン - on)
"最近、夜なかなか寝付けないんだよね。" "ん？もしかして、ふとんのスイッチが『ON』になってるんじゃない？" (The pun is that futon sounds like "ふと ON", implying a switch is on, preventing sleep. It's a bit abstract.)
Let's try a classic food-related pun that's a bit silly. Consider "イカ" (ika - squid). "イカがですか？" (Ika ga desu ka? - How is it? / How is the squid?) - This is too obvious a play on words.
How about something related to "梅" (ume - plum)? "梅雨" (tsuyu - rainy season) "梅" (ume - plum)
"最近、梅雨でジメジメするね。" "そうだな。でも、この時期は梅干しが美味しいから、まあ『梅』っちゃ『梅』かな！" (梅 (ume) and うめー (umee - delicious). The "梅" in "梅っちゃ梅かな" sounds like "うめー" meaning delicious.)
This one is fairly standard and relies on a common sound pun. "梅雨でジメジメするね。" (It's humid with the rainy season, isn't it?) "そうだな。でも、こんな天気でも気分は『うめー』ってわけにはいかないか！" (Ume- is delicious, but it doesn't quite fit "ume- desu ne" in the context of weather.)
Let's refine the "ume" idea. "最近、梅雨で洗濯物が乾かないね。" (Recently, during the rainy season, laundry doesn't dry.) "そうだな、まさに『梅ぇ』ことなしだ！" (Umē koto nashi da! - There's nothing delicious/good about it!) Here, "梅ぇ" (umē) is a pun on both "梅雨" (tsuyu, the rainy season which *contains* 梅) and "うめぇ" (delicious/good). "ことなし" means "nothing good about it".
This is getting closer. "梅ぇ" sounds like "うまい" (umai - delicious/good). "梅雨" (tsuyu - rainy season) and "梅" (ume - plum, the first character of tsuyu).
Let's try this: 「最近、梅雨でジメジメして、気分が上がらないな～」 「そうだな。こんな天気でも、気分だけは『梅ぇ！』って言いたいもんだな！」 (梅ぇ！ (umē!) sounds like delicious, but it's a pun with 梅 (ume) from 梅雨 (tsuyu). The punchline is that despite the weather, one wants to feel "delicious/good.")
It might be a bit too complex for a *simple* oyaji gag.
Let's go back to something very straightforward. A play on words with a common activity or object.
What about something related to "歯医者" (haisha - dentist)? 「歯医者さんって、いつも歯のこと考えてるんだよね。」 「うん。だから、口を開いてると『歯』って言われるんだ！」 (No, not good)
What about "コーヒー" (coffee)? 「最近、寝不足でいつも眠いんだよね。」 「それじゃあ、コーヒーを飲んで、目を『覚まコーヒー』しないとね！」 (覚まコーヒー (samakōhī) sounds like 覚まそう (samasou - let's wake up) + コーヒー (coffee). This is a classic pun style.)
This one is pretty good. It's a pun on a common word, a common situation, and uses a bit of katakana twist.
Let's try another one just in case. "ゾウ" (zō - elephant) "象が踏んでも壊れない筆箱" (A pencil case an elephant steps on and it doesn't break) - This is an old commercial tagline, not a gag.
How about something using "布団" again? 「最近、ふとんに入ると、すぐ寝ちゃうんだよね。」 「おやおや、まるで『ふと、寝る』って感じだね！」 (ふと、寝る (futo, neru - suddenly, sleep). 布団 (futon) sounds like ふとん (futo-n). This is simple and direct.)
I think this "ふとん" one is a good candidate. It's a simple pun on "ふとん" (futon) sounding like "ふと寝る" (futo neru - suddenly sleep). It's short, relatable, and uses a common item.
Let's try to format it as a typical oyaji gag. A setup and a punchline.
Setup: 「最近、寝つきがいいんだよね。」 (Recently, I fall asleep easily.) Punchline: 「ああ、布団に入ると、ふと『ん』って言ってるからかな！」 (Ah, maybe it's because when you get into the futon, you suddenly say 'ん'!) - This is weaker.
Let's go with the "ふと、寝る" (futo, neru) meaning.
Setup: 「最近、寝つきが良すぎて困ってるんだ。」 (Recently, I'm having trouble because I fall asleep too easily.) Punchline: 「そりゃ、布団が『ふと、寝る』って言ってるからじゃない？」 (Well, isn't it because your futon is telling you to "suddenly sleep"?)
This is a pretty solid oyaji gag. It uses a common word, a common situation, and a simple pun. The user wants only the text of the gag.
Okay, let's craft the final output. The prompt asks for *one* gag. I will use the "ふとん" one.
Sentence 1: Setup Sentence 2: Punchline (pun)
「最近、寝つきが良すぎて困ってるんだ。」 「そりゃ、布団が『ふと、寝る』って言ってるからじゃない？」
This is a two-line gag. I will output these two lines. No introductory phrase like "ここにギャグをどうぞ" or "今日のギャグはこれです". Just the text.

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 1067
item.description: イルカはどんな時に怒るの？ いるかいないか、はっきりしない時！

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 1192
item.description: このイカ、新鮮だね！イカだけに、いかがなものかと。

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 12
item.description: SELECT * FROM users WHERE age > 60 AND interests LIKE '%dad_jokes%';

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-12) 29
item.description:
THOUGHTS: The user wants a funny "oyaji-gyagu" (dad joke) in Japanese. I need to generate just the joke text, no extra explanation or pleasantries. It should be one joke.
Let's brainstorm some typical dad joke tropes in Japanese: - Puns on similar-sounding words (douon異義語) - Food-related puns - Animal-related puns - Situation-based puns
Example ideas: 1. **電車とパン (Train and bread)**: 電車がなかなか来ないね…まさか「食パン」してるんじゃ？ (Is the train not coming? Could it be "shokupan" (eating bread/bread factory) - a pun on "shokuhin" (food) or just a silly idea.) -> maybe too obscure. 2. **猫とトイレ (Cat and toilet)**: 猫がお風呂に入らないって？そりゃ「シャンプー」いやだもんね！ (Cat won't take a bath? Well, it hates "shampoo"! (pun on "shanpuu" and maybe "sanpuu" (stroll) or just the silliness)) -> "シャンプー" (shampoo) is good. 3. **ペンとインク (Pen and ink)**: ペンが疲れたって？インク切れじゃなくて「やる気」切れだね！ (Pen is tired? Not out of ink, but out of "motivation"!) -> A bit too abstract. 4. **アイスと寒い (Ice cream and cold)**: 「寒いからアイス食べない？」って聞かれたけど、それは「逆」だろ！ (Someone asked "It's cold, wanna eat ice cream?" but that's "the opposite"!) -> This one is a classic anti-joke structure. 5. **トイレとハワイ (Toilet and Hawaii)**: トイレに行きたいけど我慢してるんだ…「ハワイ」に行けないのが辛いよ！ (I want to go to the toilet but I'm holding it... It's hard not being able to go to "Hawaii"!) -> This is a classic one! ハワイ (Hawaii) and 葉は良い (ha wa ii - leaves are good/ha yai - hurry up). No, the common one is 我慢 (gaman - endure) and ハワイ (Hawaii). The punchline is usually "ハワイにイカない (ikana-i)" - "I don't go to Hawaii" / "Squid don't go to Hawaii". Or "ハワイ行きたい" (I want to go to Hawaii) when you want to go to the bathroom. Let's try: トイレを我慢してるんだ。早く「ハワイ」に行きたいよ… (I'm holding it. I want to go to "Hawaii" quickly...) -> "ハワイに行きたい" is quite common. The pun is "はやく行きたい" (hayaku ikitai - want to go quickly) -> "ハワイに行きたい". It implies the person wants to go to the toilet quickly.
Let's refine the Hawaii one. "トイレを我慢してるんだ。早く「ハワイ」に行きたいよ…" This is a good, classic, slightly groan-worthy dad joke.
Another option: 「この鉛筆、短いなぁ。」「もう『芯』がないね！」 (This pencil is short. "There's no core left!" / "It has no 'shin' (core/heart/truth)" -> "shin ga nai" can mean no core, or no truth, or no strength etc. This is a bit weak for a dad joke.)
How about something with food? 「パン屋さんのアルバイト、疲れたなぁ。」「でも、『パン』に愛着が湧いたんじゃない？」 (I'm tired from my part-time job at the bakery. But didn't you grow to love "pan" (bread)?) -> "パン" (bread) and "愛着" (affection). Maybe a pun on "パンと愛" (bread and love). Not punchy enough.
Let's stick with the toilet/Hawaii one. It's a well-known archetype. It fulfills the requirements: - Funny (in a dad joke way) - Oyaji-gyagu - Japanese - Single text line - No extra info
Final check: トイレを我慢してるんだ。早く「ハワイ」に行きたいよ…。 It's simple, a bit silly, and uses a common pun often associated with dad jokes.

item.title: 今日のギャグ (2026-06-11) 1156
item.description: 考えすぎたプログラマーが言いました。「今日の残業、"while(true)"って感じ？」
